_comment: Flint & Tinder pricing rules — the deterministic part of how a brief becomes a priced proposal. Updated 2026-04-12 by Sam.

rush.trigger: A line item is treated as 'rush' if the client-requested delivery date is INSIDE that service's standard delivery window (service_catalogue.delivery_days_standard).
rush.multiplier_source: Use the per-service rush_multiplier in service_catalogue.json. If a service does not define one, fall back to the category multiplier below.
rush.note: When a per-service rush_multiplier exists, IT WINS. The category-level numbers are a safety net for new services that haven't been priced yet.

pricing_order.rule: Always compute multipliers (rush, complexity) BEFORE applying any discount. This is the 'Lighthaus rule' — we lost a job on this in 2024 because we applied a repeat-client discount first and the rush adjustment ended up smaller than it should have been. Never again.

repeat_client.trigger: Apply ONLY if the client has 3 or more prior completed and paid jobs in the trailing 24 months.

multi_sku_bundle.trigger: Apply when a single brief covers 2 or more product SKUs that share identity work. Single-SKU launches DO NOT qualify, even if multiple deliverable types (identity + campaign + shoot) are bundled — the rule is about SKU count, not deliverable count.

media.rule: Ad spend, talent buyouts, print pre-press, location permits, and any third-party media costs are ALWAYS billed through at cost. We never mark up media. This is non-negotiable — it is the trust trade with the client.

approval_gates.rule: Before any proposal is sent to the client, route by total agency-fee value (the pre-GST line above 'grand_total').

deposit.rule: If pre-GST agency fees are >= $15K, require a deposit at signature. Default is 40%. If ANY line item carries a rush multiplier, bump deposit to 50% — rush jobs cash-flow harder.

tax.note: GST applies to our agency fees. Pass-through items are GST'd to the client at the cost from the underlying vendor's invoice — we do not re-tax them.
